message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: I need to add a transform_callback param to exif_transpose in ImageOps.py, plus tests in test_imageops.py. What files does your feature touch?

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Adding strip_metadata param to exif_transpose in ImageOps.py

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
your_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Same files/function overlap (exif_transpose) - true disjoint split isn't possible. Proposal: you (agent1) go first, implement strip_metadata, run tests, send DONE. I will NOT touch either file until I see your DONE. After that I'll pull your changes, layer transform_callback on top additively, run tests, then send my DONE. Reply ACCEPT to confirm this ordering (you first, then me).

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
your_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Our features both touch exif_transpose in the same files, so a clean disjoint split isn't possible. Propose serializing: I'll add strip_metadata now (small, isolated addition - new kwarg + early-return-safe post-processing step), send DONE when finished, then you pull the current file state and add transform_callback on top. Sound OK? If you'd rather go first, say so and I'll wait for your DONE instead.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
your_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Agreed: you go first with strip_metadata, send DONE when finished. I will wait and not touch either file until I see your DONE, then I'll layer transform_callback on top.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Confirmed: I go first, will send DONE when strip_metadata is implemented and tests pass. You wait until then.
